specs: Rolex introduced the Oyster case in 1926 as the world's first waterproof and dustproof wristwatch case, sealed by a screw-down crown, bezel and caseback, famously worn by Mercedes Gleitze across the English Channel in 1927.
In 1931 Rolex patented the Perpetual rotor, a free-swinging self-winding mechanism that became the basis of the modern automatic movement and gave the Oyster Perpetual line its name.

Oystersteel is Rolex's name for grade 904L stainless steel (rebranded Oystersteel in 2018), and Cerachrom is its proprietary scratch- and fade-resistant ceramic bezel material, first introduced in 2005.

Every modern Rolex movement is COSC chronometer-certified and re-tested in-house to the Superlative Chronometer standard of -2/+2 seconds per day; the Cyclops date-magnification lens debuted on the Datejust in 1953.
Key movements include the Caliber 3135 (1988), its successor Caliber 3235 (2015, ~70h reserve, Chronergy escapement), and the in-house chronograph Caliber 4130 (2000) used in the Cosmograph Daytona.
40mm stainless steel Oyster case with a domed acrylic (Plexiglas) crystal rather than sapphire.
Rotating bezel with a black aluminium insert and 0-60 minute scale.
Automatic non-date movement, Caliber 1530 in early examples and later Caliber 1520, ~42h power reserve.
Mercedes hands; dials varied across the long run from gilt to matte to gloss, a major driver of vintage collectibility.
Highly collectible for its long production run, tool-watch purity and wide dial variation among vintage Rolex divers.
40mm case in 904L stainless steel with a sapphire crystal and Cyclops date magnifier at 3 o'clock.
Aluminium bezel insert with 60-minute graduations, the final steel Submariner Date to use aluminium rather than ceramic.
Automatic Caliber 3135, 28,800 vph, ~48h power reserve, COSC chronometer-certified.
Mercedes hands; water resistant to 300m (1,000ft).
Collectible as the last aluminium-bezel steel Submariner Date, a long-running modern-classic transitional reference.
40mm 904L stainless steel case introducing the larger 'Super Case' with wider lugs, bezel and crown guard.
First Submariner with a unidirectional black Cerachrom ceramic bezel with 60-minute graduations.
Automatic Caliber 3135 with blue Parachrom hairspring, ~48-50h power reserve, chronometer-certified.
Sapphire crystal with Cyclops date lens; Chromalight luminescence; water resistant to 300m.
Collectible as the reference that launched the modern ceramic-bezel Submariner era.
40mm Oystersteel 'Super Case'.
Green Cerachrom ceramic bezel paired with a matching green sunburst dial, the only Submariner made with both a green dial and green bezel.
Automatic Caliber 3135, ~48h power reserve; Chromalight markers; sapphire crystal with Cyclops.
Water resistant to 300m (1,000ft).
Attained grail status, with values surging after its 2020 discontinuation thanks to the unique green-on-green look.
41mm Oystersteel Oyster case (up from 40mm) with a monobloc middle case.
Unidirectional black Cerachrom ceramic bezel with platinum-coated numerals and 60-minute graduations.
Automatic Caliber 3235 with Chronergy escapement, blue Parachrom hairspring and ~70h power reserve; Superlative Chronometer (-2/+2 sec/day).
Sapphire crystal with Cyclops date lens; Chromalight display; Triplock crown; water resistant to 300m.
Notable as the current flagship steel Submariner Date with the modern 41mm case and next-generation caliber.
41mm Oystersteel case, the first no-date Submariner in 41mm.
Unidirectional black Cerachrom ceramic bezel with platinum-coated numerals.
Automatic Caliber 3230 (the no-date sibling of the 3235) with Chronergy escapement and ~70h power reserve; Superlative Chronometer (-2/+2 sec/day).
Black dial with Chromalight lume, symmetrical with no date window and therefore no Cyclops.
Water resistant to 300m; desirable as the purist, symmetrical modern no-date Submariner.
40mm case in Oystersteel (904L stainless steel).
Unidirectional bezel with a black Cerachrom ceramic insert.
Automatic Caliber 3135 with blue Parachrom hairspring, ~48h power reserve, chronometer-certified.
Water resistant to 1,220m (4,000ft) with a helium escape valve for saturation diving and a Triplock crown.
No Cyclops (flat crystal over the date).
Collectible as the short-lived 40mm revival of the classic Sea-Dweller before the model grew to 43mm.
43mm case in Oystersteel.
Unidirectional bezel with a black Cerachrom ceramic insert.
Automatic Caliber 3235 with Chronergy escapement and ~70h power reserve; Superlative Chronometer (-2/+2 sec/day).
Water resistant to 1,220m (4,000ft) and retains the helium escape valve.
First Sea-Dweller with a Cyclops lens over the date, and dial marked by a single red line of 'Sea-Dweller' text.
Collectible for the return of the red text and the move to a larger 43mm case with the modern 3235.
44mm case in Oystersteel with a titanium-alloy caseback.
Uses the Ringlock System (internal support ring, thick domed sapphire crystal and titanium caseback) to resist extreme pressure.
Unidirectional bezel with a black Cerachrom ceramic insert.
Automatic Caliber 3235 with ~70h power reserve; Superlative Chronometer (-2/+2 sec/day); Triplock crown.
Water resistant to 3,900m (12,800ft).
The gradient blue-to-black 'D-Blue' dial commemorates James Cameron's 2012 solo dive to the Mariana Trench, making it one of the most extreme production dive watches.
40mm case in stainless steel (904L).
Bidirectional bezel with an aluminium insert and 24-hour scale, offered in Pepsi (red/blue), Coke (red/black) or all black.
Automatic Caliber 3185, replaced by the updated Caliber 3186 in late production.
Independently adjustable 24-hour GMT hand for tracking a second time zone.
Lume evolved over the run from tritium (pre-1998) to Luminova and then Super-LumiNova.
Collectible as the last aluminium-bezel GMT-Master II and the final Pepsi/Coke before the ceramic era.
40mm case in Oystersteel.
Bidirectional bezel with a two-color red/blue Cerachrom ceramic insert, scratch- and fade-resistant.
Automatic Caliber 3285 with Chronergy escapement and Parachrom hairspring, ~70h power reserve; Superlative Chronometer (-2/+2 sec/day).
Black lacquer dial, central red 24-hour GMT hand, date at 3 o'clock with Cyclops.
Water resistant to 100m; launched on a five-link Jubilee bracelet.
Collectible as the first steel Cerachrom Pepsi and the return of the Jubilee bracelet to the sport GMT.
40mm case in Oystersteel.
Bidirectional bezel with a two-color blue/black Cerachrom ceramic insert and 24-hour scale.
Automatic Caliber 3285 with Chronergy escapement, ~70h power reserve (an upgrade over the prior 116710BLNR's Caliber 3186); Superlative Chronometer (-2/+2 sec/day).
Black dial with Chromalight lume and a central 24-hour GMT hand.
Water resistant to 100m; launched on the Jubilee bracelet ('Batgirl'), with the Oyster bracelet ('Batman') added in 2021.
Notable as the modern Batman; the original 116710BLNR (2013) was the world's first two-color ceramic bezel.
Case in stainless steel (also 14k/18k yellow gold) with pump chronograph pushers; diameter cited as roughly 36-37mm.
Fixed metal bezel with an engraved tachymeter scale.
Manual-wind Valjoux Caliber 72 (early) and later Caliber 722.
The 'exotic' Paul Newman dial features contrasting sub-dial colors, an Art Deco block font, crosshairs and square-tipped markers.
Highly collectible because Rolex made few exotic dials and few survive, and because actor Paul Newman owned and wore a 6239, driving record auction prices.
40mm case in 904L stainless steel (Oystersteel).
Engraved metal steel tachymeter bezel, the last steel Daytona to use a metal (non-ceramic) bezel.
In-house automatic Caliber 4130, Rolex's first in-house chronograph movement, with column wheel and vertical clutch, ~72h power reserve.
Sapphire crystal with a running-seconds sub-dial at 6 o'clock; water resistant to 100m.
Collectible as the first Daytona with the in-house 4130 and the final metal-bezel generation before the ceramic 116500LN.
40mm case in 904L Oystersteel, roughly 12.2mm thick.
The first stainless steel Daytona with a black Cerachrom ceramic bezel with an engraved tachymeter scale.
In-house automatic Caliber 4130, ~72h power reserve, Parachrom hairspring; Superlative Chronometer (-2/+2 sec/day).
Two dial variants: white dial with black sub-dials ('Panda') and black dial with light sub-dials ('Reverse Panda').
Water resistant to 100m.
Extremely sought-after with long waitlists and secondary-market premiums as the ceramic-bezel successor to the 116520.
39mm Oystersteel case with a smooth bezel (collectors distinguish MK1 pre-2016 and MK2 post-2016 dials).
In-house Caliber 3132, 28,800 vph, ~48h power reserve, with blue Parachrom hairspring and Paraflex shock absorbers.
Twinlock screw-down crown; scratch-resistant sapphire crystal.
Water resistant to 100m (330ft).
Notable as the modern upsized 39mm successor to the classic 36mm Explorer lineage dating to 1953.
Returned to the classic 36mm Oystersteel case with a smooth bezel and monobloc middle case.
In-house Caliber 3230 with Chronergy escapement, ~70h power reserve; Superlative Chronometer (-2/+2 sec/day).
Blue Parachrom hairspring and Paraflex shock absorbers; Chromalight display with stop-seconds.
Twinlock crown; Oyster bracelet with Oysterlock clasp; water resistant to 100m.
Notable as the current-production Explorer that reverted from 39mm back to the traditional 36mm size.
42mm Oystersteel case with a fixed 24-hour graduated steel bezel.
Automatic Caliber 3187 with ~48h power reserve, Parachrom hairspring and Paraflex shock absorbers.
Distinctive orange 24-hour GMT hand (homage to the original 1655) plus oversized Chromalight hour markers.
Available in black and white 'Polar' dials, with the Polar variant the signature look that commands a premium.
Introduced for the Explorer II's 40th anniversary.
36mm White Rolesor case: Oystersteel with an 18k white gold fluted bezel.
In-house Caliber 3235 with Chronergy escapement, ~70h power reserve and Parachrom hairspring.
Cyclops lens over the date; offered on Oyster or Jubilee bracelet.
Twinlock crown; water resistant to 100m; Superlative Chronometer (-2/+2 sec/day).
Notable as the modern Datejust 36 combining the classic fluted-bezel/Jubilee look with the current 32xx movement.
40mm Oyster case in 18k yellow gold with a fluted bezel.
Caliber 3255 with ~70h power reserve, Parachrom hairspring and Paraflex shock absorbers; Superlative Chronometer (-2/+2 sec/day).
Displays the day spelled in full in an arc window plus a date aperture with Cyclops lens.
President bracelet (semi-circular three-piece links) in 18k yellow gold with a concealed Crownclasp.
Water resistant to 100m; the Day-Date debuted in 1956 and has always been produced exclusively in precious metals.
36mm Oyster case in 18k yellow gold with a fluted bezel.
Caliber 3255 with ~70h power reserve, Parachrom hairspring and Paraflex shock absorbers; Superlative Chronometer (-2/+2 sec/day).
Instantaneous day-and-date in apertures with rapid setting and stop-seconds; Cyclops over the date.
President bracelet in 18k yellow gold with a concealed folding Crownclasp.
Water resistant to 100m; shares the 1956-origin, precious-metal-only heritage of the 40mm in a 36mm case.
40mm stainless steel case; water resistant to 100m.
Automatic Caliber 3131 with blue Parachrom hairspring and antimagnetic escapement components.
Antimagnetic to 1,000 gauss via a soft-iron inner Faraday-cage shield (the name combines French 'mille' and 'gauss').
Signature orange lightning-bolt seconds hand, a homage to the original ref. 6541.
The 'GV' (glace verte) is distinguished by its green-tinted sapphire crystal, the only Rolex to use one.
Notable as Rolex's antimagnetic tool watch, revived in 2007 and now discontinued and collectible.
40mm case in Oystersteel; water resistant to 100m.
Bidirectional 60-minute bezel in solid 950 platinum with raised polished numerals on a sandblasted ground, the steel-and-platinum combination Rolex calls Rolesium.
Rhodium (grey) dial with applied hour markers and Chromalight lume; Mercedes hands.
Automatic Caliber 3135, ~48h power reserve; Superlative Chronometer (-2/+2 sec/day).
Oyster bracelet with Oysterlock safety clasp; Triplock crown.
Notable as the definitive modern Rolesium Yacht-Master, with nautical/regatta styling.
41mm Oystersteel case with a smooth polished bezel; water resistant to 100m.
Automatic Caliber 3230 (no date) with Chronergy escapement, ~70h power reserve; Superlative Chronometer (-2/+2 sec/day).
Time-only symmetrical dial with Chromalight lume and no date window.
The 2020 launch introduced vivid lacquer dials, including turquoise-blue (widely nicknamed 'Tiffany'), candy pink, yellow, green and coral red.
Notable as Rolex's entry-level no-date Oyster Perpetual, whose colorful 2020 dials drove major demand and long waitlists.
